title: Senior Backend Developers and Engineers - Automated Disbursement & Agentic Workflows

contact_persons: Coral Ramsey - primary payments/disbursements owner for 3 roles
client_overview.business_domain: Backend / Grants / Nonprofit Payouts / Agentic AI

roles_descriptions.title: Senior Backend Developer - Automated Disbursement Service (net-new greenfield)
roles_descriptions.description: Build AWS serverless workflows, DAF/payout integrations, reconciliation automation, and nonprofit redirection logic.

roles_descriptions.title: Senior Software Engineer - CyberGrants Disbursement Service (CGDS)
roles_descriptions.description: Remediate legacy Node / PL/SQL / Oracle workflows, reduce manual production DB access, build safer Ops/CS tooling, prioritize high-ROI security/bug/performance fixes.

roles_descriptions.title: Senior/Expert Software Engineer - CyberGrants Q / Agentic Workflows
roles_descriptions.description: Integrate CyberGrants product APIs into internal agent framework, add product-specific skills/tools, bridge modern serverless TypeScript work with legacy CyberGrants APIs.

roles_descriptions.title: DevOps Engineer - CyberGrants Environment/Build Tooling
roles_descriptions.description: Standardize legacy CyberGrants environments, long-lived environments, customer-specific customizations, build/tooling standardization. Pending deeper definition with DevOps lead.

technical_profile.company_description: Client/product context: grants, corporate giving, donor-advised funds (DAFs), nonprofit payouts, and CyberGrants products. The systems move real money and sensitive financial data. One legacy system was described as moving about half a billion dollars per year. The client is building a new automated disbursement service while also stabilizing legacy CyberGrants/CGDS systems and expanding agentic AI capabilities.

example_features: Automated disbursement and bank/spreadsheet reconciliation to reduce manual ops dependency
Redirection logic when a nonprofit loses legal/eligible status before payout
DAF and payout integrations, including internal DAF and PayPal Giving Fund context
Event-driven workflow orchestration, traceability, recovery/debuggability for Lambda-based flows
Ops/CS interfaces that prevent non-technical users from directly editing financial production tables
Agentic AI 'skills' that call product APIs, use product data, and perform customer-authorized work inside the application
process_with_client: Tikal to send candidates as soon as possible. Recommended interview prep: candidate should understand the latest notes and sync briefly with the last Tikal member who interviewed there.

gl_feedback.nir_alfasi: אני חושב שזו היתה טעות מצד בונטרה לעשות את השיחות עם ארכיטקטים ולא עם האדם אליו הם אמורים לדווח. הארכיטקטים לא עשו רושם טוב: נשמע שהם לא סגורים על עצמם ועל מה שצריך לעשות, הם רק סגורים על זה שהם רוצים שהפרוייקט יהיה גמור בתחילת יולי. שזה קצת מגוחך. שנית, הם לא ידעו לענות על שום שאלה שקשורה לשגרת הפיתוח: עושה רושם שהם אמורים לעבוד מול פיר אמריקאי (אחד מול אחד) כך שתהיה על כל משימה עבודה שמכוסה רוב שעות היום. התקשורת אמורה להיות רק דרך פיארים. זו תוכנית גרועה מאוד.

interview_intelligence.asaf_varon_feedback: They are midlevel engineers. They probably could be productive with enough support and help, but aren't going to jump in and drive things the way we need. No substantive AWS knowledge. Lack of architectural/systems level thinking. Very standard / basic AI.
interview_intelligence.recommendations: This role is focused on a greenfield backend disbursement system for automating grant payments from corporations to nonprofits via DAF providers. The client emphasized strong backend ownership, AWS/serverless experience, TypeScript, Postgres, EventBridge, and comfort working on financial/risk-sensitive systems.
